title: age-group — retention windows
description: AgeGroup rows follow a soft-delete → archived → hard-delete lifecycle. The hold window is 1095 days (3 years) matching rolling youth-development cohort windows so an archived age group stays recoverable across a full cohort's lifetime. Audit rows produced by this module outlive their source AgeGroup for 7 years (minor-safeguarding retention floor).

cold.days: age_group.retention.archived_age_groups_retention_days (default 1095 — 3 years; up to 2555 per Enterprise tenant setting)
cold.state: deleted_at set (soft-deleted); hidden from tenant selectors + resolver; readable by admins via `?include_archived=true`; inbound rows continue to point at it during the window (Team + EventDivision may hold FKs, AthleteEnrollment snapshots continue to work regardless).

purge.trigger: deleted_at + age_group.retention.archived_age_groups_retention_days past AND no inbound row references (Team, EventDivision; snapshots don't count)
purge.note: IF any inbound row (Team, EventDivision, TeamRegistration) still references the AgeGroup past the retention window, the job SKIPS the row + logs a telemetry counter (`academorix.age_group.purge_pending.count`). The row survives another day. Explicit `age-group:archive --force` bypasses the FK check but does NOT delete referring rows.

cold.days: up to 2555 (7 years — minor-safeguarding retention floor)

purge.action: audit rows OUTLIVE their AgeGroup — that's the compliance signal for minor-safeguarding replays. Retention is set by `audit::config.retention_days` clamped up by the compliance floor for minor-safeguarding records (7 years).

purge.trigger: TTL expiry OR invalidation on AgeGroup mutation

notes: The 1095-day hold window is the DEFAULT. Enterprise tenants may extend up to 2555 days (7 years) via the tenant setting — matching the audit-row retention so archived AgeGroups and their audits both stay recoverable through the same window.

Hard-delete of an in-use AgeGroup is IMPOSSIBLE by design. The AgeGroupObserver + FK RESTRICT combine to keep the AgeGroup alive as long as any Team / EventDivision references it. This is intentional — an AgeGroup hard-delete would invalidate the eligibility trail on every enrollment that transited through it.

AthleteEnrollment references via `age_group_snapshot_id` do NOT block delete. Snapshots are self-contained copies of the bounds + cutoff at enrollment time; the source AgeGroup can be safely deleted once no non-snapshot references remain.

The compliance floor for minor-safeguarding audit rows is 7 years (2555 days) regardless of tenant tier. This is the compliance floor, not a tenant setting — every AgeGroup mutation audit row survives at least 7 years past its source AgeGroup's hard-delete.
